reason=Bash tool unusable in this execution worktree — every invocation fails with ENOSPC: no space left on device, mkdir '/tmp/claude-501/-Users-erik-Projects--ddx-exec-wt--execute-bead-wt-ddx-c4497391-20260713T161219-a8ec3d37' This is host /tmp exhaustion breaking the agent's own Bash sandbox tracking directory, not a repo or bead issue. Confirmed persistent (retried `true` and `df -h` with dangerouslyDisableSandbox=true; both fail identically). Without a working Bash tool there is no way to run `go build`, `go test`, `git`, or `lefthook run pre-commit` in this worktree, so none of ddx-c4497391's ACs can be produced or verified here. Read/Write/Edit tools still function (this file and the source-file read above succeeded), but they cannot substitute for the required verification commands. This matches a previously observed failure class: host /tmp exhaustion can kill the agent's own Bash tool, independent of any ddx binary bug. No code change was made — the fix belongs at the host level (free space under /tmp) or in a fresh worktree/machine with available /tmp space, not in this bead's in-scope files.
suggested_action=recheck the external blocker and move status to open when cleared
